message:
feat(aws): block athena file:// and --cli-input-file bypass classes

Review-pass follow-up on 17a54e3. Two bypass classes remained on
Athena even after the global-flags fix, because the destructive SQL
is loaded from disk/opaque input and never hits the command line:

1. `aws athena start-query-execution --query-string file:///tmp/q.sql`
   — AWS CLI's `file://` (and `fileb://`) protocol reads the SQL from
   a file, so the DROP/TRUNCATE/unscoped-DELETE regexes downstream
   have nothing to grep.

2. `aws athena start-query-execution --cli-input-json file://input.json`
   (same for `--cli-input-yaml`) — loads the whole invocation from a
   file, hiding `QueryString` from per-parameter inspection.

Two new High-severity rules:

- `athena-query-string-from-file` — blocks `file://`/`fileb://` on
  `--query-string`. Users who actually need file-backed queries
  (long read-only reports) can allowlist this specific rule.

- `athena-cli-input-file` — blocks `--cli-input-json file://…` and
  `--cli-input-yaml file://…` specifically. **Inline** JSON/YAML
  (`--cli-input-json '{…}'`) is deliberately *not* blocked by this
  rule — the inline blob is visible on the command line, so a DROP
  or TRUNCATE buried inside still trips the broader Athena rules.
  Over-blocking inline would have annoyed users who legitimately
  pass small inline config.

Regression coverage:
- `athena_query_string_via_file_protocol_is_flagged` covers file://,
  fileb://, --cli-input-json file://, --cli-input-yaml file://, and
  the file:// shape behind AWS global flags.
- `athena_cli_input_inline_json_still_grepped_by_existing_rules`
  asserts inline safe JSON is allowed, and inline DROP DATABASE JSON
  is still caught by the existing broad DROP rule — proving the
  narrowed `athena-cli-input-file` doesn't leak coverage.

Full suite: 2,129 passed, 0 failed.

files.patch:
@@ -76,6 +76,8 @@ These patterns match potentially destructive commands:
 | `athena-query-drop-database` | Athena `DROP DATABASE`/`SCHEMA` removes the database from the Glue catalog. | critical |
 | `athena-query-drop-table` | Athena `DROP TABLE`/`VIEW` removes the table definition from the Glue catalog. | high |
 | `athena-query-truncate` | Athena `TRUNCATE TABLE` deletes all rows from an Iceberg table. | critical |
+| `athena-query-string-from-file` | Athena `--query-string file://…`/`fileb://…` loads the SQL from disk, so DCG can't grep the statement. Use inline `--query-string '…'` instead. | high |
+| `athena-cli-input-file` | Athena `start-query-execution --cli-input-json file://…`/`--cli-input-yaml file://…` loads the full invocation from disk, hiding `QueryString` from inspection. Inline `--cli-input-json '{…}'` is still allowed because the broad `DROP`/`TRUNCATE`/`DELETE` rules can still see its contents. | high |
 | `athena-query-delete-without-where` | Athena `DELETE` without a `WHERE` clause removes all rows from the target table. | critical |
 | `glue-delete-database` | aws glue delete-database removes the database and every table definition inside it. | critical |
 | `glue-delete-table` | aws glue delete-table removes the table definition from the catalog. | high |
